name: Transparency as underserved quality gap
description: Participants consistently identified explanation quality and audit trail completeness as the most underserved dimension in their current tooling. This gap was felt acutely in regulated sectors.
key_evidence: Audit trails exist but are unstructured and unqueryable (INT-01, INT-07, INT-13)
User trust erosion when outputs cannot be explained — users stop relying on the system (INT-04, INT-10)
Regulatory explanation gap: EU AI Act, HIPAA, and financial regulation all require explainability (INT-07, INT-08, INT-13, INT-14)
significance_for_qalis: Second most common theme (n=48). Paper finding: TI lowest-scoring QALIS dimension (mean 7.05). 11/14 participants cited transparency gap unprompted. Directly motivates TI dimension.
